questions.text: Explain the difference between processes and threads. When would you choose one over the other?
Walk me through how a hash map works under the hood. What happens when there is a collision?
What is the difference between TCP and UDP? Give a real-world example where you would pick one over the other.
Compare optimistic and pessimistic concurrency control. When does each break down?
Walk me through what happens, end to end, when a user types a URL into a browser and hits enter.
Design a URL shortener. Walk me through the data model, the read path, and how you would scale it to a billion URLs.
Design a real-time chat application. How would you handle message ordering, delivery guarantees, and offline users?
Design a rate limiter for an API gateway. Compare token bucket, leaky bucket, and sliding window approaches.
Design Twitter's home timeline. How do you handle fan-out for a celebrity with 100 million followers?
Design a distributed key-value store with strong consistency. How does your design handle network partitions?
Design a global content delivery network. How do you decide which edge node serves a given user?
Design a notification service that can deliver push, email, and SMS at a rate of one million per minute.
Design a payment system. What are the consistency and idempotency requirements, and how do you enforce them?
Explain CAP theorem. Walk through a real system you have worked on and the trade-off it made.
Describe how an L1 cache, L2 cache, and main memory differ, and how that affects how you write performance-sensitive code.
Describe what happens when you call an async function in your language of choice. Where does the work actually run?
Walk me through how you would design a retry policy for an HTTP client that talks to an external API.
How do you decide between a microservice and a modular monolith for a new product team of five engineers?
What is the difference between unit, integration, and end-to-end tests? How do you decide what to cover at each level?
How would you debug a memory leak in a long-running service that you only see in production?
Explain the difference between SQL transaction isolation levels. When would read committed cause a real bug?
How does HTTPS work under the hood? What does the TLS handshake actually do?
Walk me through how you would migrate a 500-million-row table from one schema to another with zero downtime.
What is the difference between idempotent and safe HTTP methods? When does it actually matter?
Describe your approach to writing a code review that strengthens both the code and the author.
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a senior engineer on a technical decision. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a project where you had to ship under significant ambiguity. How did you decide what was good enough?
Tell me about a time you made a technical decision that turned out to be wrong. What did you do about it?
Tell me about a time you had to push back on a product manager's scope. What was the outcome?
Tell me about a time you drove a project independently without being asked to. What was the impact?
Tell me about a time you received critical feedback that was hard to hear. How did you respond?
Tell me about a time a project missed its deadline. What did you change for the next one?
Tell me about a production incident you led the response to. What did you learn?
Describe a situation where you had to work with a difficult teammate. How did you keep the project on track?
How do you prioritise when you have multiple urgent tasks from different stakeholders?
Describe a time you mentored a junior engineer through a difficult problem. What approach did you take?
Tell me about a time you had to learn a new technology on a tight deadline. How did you approach it?
Describe how you have advocated for paying down technical debt when product wanted only features.
What language do you reach for first for a new side project, and why?
Walk me through your process for building a simple CRUD API from scratch.
